title: desmodium maxonii
intro: Desmodium maxonii, also known as the Brazilian clover or maxon's tick trefoil, is a leguminous vine native to Brazil and other parts of South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Desmodium maxonii include treating skin conditions, wounds, and fungal infections, as well as being used as an antihelmintic to treat parasitic worms. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
